rationale: Empirically reproduced the headline against a scratch .mindspec/config.yaml encoding the standing four-family protocol (spec/plan 3F+3O+3G=9, bead 3O+3S+1F+1G=8, final four-family=12, threshold '8'/'8'/'n-1'/'11'). `config show` renders all 4 configured gates in enum order with matching resolved sums/raw thresholds; `--gate <g> --json` yields exactly the 5 documented members via real encoding/json; adhoc (unconfigured) slots equal bead's slots byte-for-byte; unknown `--gate zzz` exits 1 with the 5-key ADR-0035 recovery line; `--json` without `--gate` is refused distinctly. Known-model advisory warns (sorted, exit 0) on injected unknown ids in gate reviewers and both substitutes sides, silent on known ones. Hostile-string probe (ESC/BEL/embedded-newline in note, a gate reviewer model, a lens, and both substitutes sides) produced zero raw control bytes on stdout in either text or JSON path (grep -c $'\x1b'/$'\x07' = 0); JSON round-tripped through jq byte-exact. Confirmed the R7 nil-guard at complete.go:386 (`gateCfgErr == nil && panelReg != nil`) matches the BRIEF's described fail-open safety. All 5 named tests green (TestConfigShow_GatesSubstitutesAndModelAdvisory, TestConfigShow_ReviewerCountNoteGateAware, TestConfigShowGate_ResolvedJSON, TestConfigShow_HostileStringsEscaped, TestPanelAdvisory_GateAwareCompare/9 subtests); cmd/mindspec, internal/complete, internal/config all green; leaf invariant holds (go list -deps ./internal/panel | grep internal/config → exit 1/no match). git status clean throughout.
findings: Headline reproduced exactly: config show sums 9/9/8/12 in PanelGateKeys enum order (spec_approve, plan_approve, bead, final_review; adhoc correctly omitted from the rendered list as unconfigured), each with its raw threshold expr ('8','8','n-1','11') printed unresolved.
config show --gate <g> --json keys are exactly [approve_threshold, expected_reviewers, gate, slots, substitution] for all 4 configured gates plus adhoc.

adhoc (not present in panel.gates) correctly falls back to bead's resolved slots (byte-identical .slots array); only the top-level 'gate' field differs ('adhoc' vs 'bead'), as the resolver contract specifies.

Unknown --gate exits 1 with 'recovery: pass one of spec_approve, plan_approve, bead, final_review, adhoc ...' (5 keys, ADR-0035 shape). --json with no --gate refused with its own distinct recovery line, also exit 1.
in_force flips correctly: 'claude_sub_on_quota' when substitutes empty, 'substitutes' the instant a substitutes map entry exists (R5 supersession).

Known-model advisory: injecting 'totally-made-up-model-x' (gate reviewer) and 'another-unknown-sub' (substitutes value) produced two sorted, escaped warning comments; exit code stayed 0; the known id claude-opus-4-8 used as a substitutes key produced no warning.

Escaping probe: note='hostile\x1b[31mnote
recovery: fake line', a gate reviewer model='evil
recovery: pwn3d', a lens with embedded ESC, and a substitutes key/value with BEL/ESC — all rendered as strconv.Quote'd single-line literals in text (zero raw 0x1b/0x07 bytes on stdout) and as //
 JSON escapes that jq round-trips byte-exact.

Minor non-blocking observation: an empty (nil) substitutes map marshals to JSON `null` rather than `{}` (Go's encoding/json nil-map behavior) even though the text path's renderSubstitutes always prints 'substitutes: {}' for the same case. The BRIEF's never-omit clause is about the text/render path and the 5-member JSON contract (key present, which it is); this is a cosmetic asymmetry, not a contract violation — flagging for awareness, not requesting a change.

Confirmed R7's nil-guard at internal/complete/complete.go:386 (`if gateCfgErr == nil && panelReg != nil { if def, ok := ... }`) exactly matches the BRIEF's described fail-open safety; reviewerCountNotesFor's `if reg.Err != nil { continue }` plus PanelGateAdvisoryDefault's ok-check is the same single selection rule reused, no drift between the two call sites.
